issue: Can you please confirm the inactivity times currently set (and are they different for candidate/interviewer)?  Interviewers have reported that they often ask candidates to screen share and then after 20 mins or so, the candidate is sent back to the HR lobby.  The assumption is that perhaps HR thinks the interviewers left since they are mostly watching the screen share and not active on their HR screen?  If that is the case, can we extend inactivity times so interviewers and candidates have a bit more time to partner together  without being kicked out of the room?
subject: Candidate inactivity help
response: HackerRank Interviews have inactivity timeout settings that may move candidates to the lobby if no activity is detected. According to the October 2025 Release Notes, candidates are automatically moved to the lobby if no interviewer joins within 3 minutes, but this does not explicitly cover inactivity during an ongoing interview. For real-time monitoring, you can use Observation Mode to observe the candidate's progress without interruption. For more details on lobby behavior, see [Using Virtual Lobby in HackerRank Interviews](https://support.hackerrank.com/articles/1151935613-using-virtual-lobby-in-hackerrank-interviews). If you need to adjust inactivity settings, please check your interview configuration or contact your account administrator.
justification: The user is asking for information about inactivity times, which is a policy/usage question. The corpus provides relevant details about lobby behavior and observation mode [Doc 2, Doc 4, Doc 5].

issue: Hello! I am trying to remove an interviewer from the platform. I am not seeing this as an option when I select the three dots next to their name.  Can you let me know how to do this?
subject: How to Remove a User
response: To remove a user's access from an interview template, follow these steps: 1. Go to **Interviews > Templates**. 2. Click the **More options (⋮)** menu next to the template and select **Share**. 3. In the **Share Interview Template** dialog box, select the user or team you want to remove. 4. Select **Remove User** from the drop-down menu. 5. Click **Confirm** in the confirmation dialog box. If you are trying to remove a user from the platform entirely, you may need to deactivate their account via **User Management** (for admins).
justification: The user can self-serve by following documented steps for removing access from an interview template [Doc 1] or deactivating a user [Doc 4].

issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
subject: Website Data crawl
response: To stop Claude from crawling your website, add the following to your robots.txt file in the top-level directory:  User-agent: ClaudeBot Disallow: /  This will block the crawler from your entire website. For more details, see [Anthropic's crawling documentation](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler).
justification: User can self-serve by modifying robots.txt as documented in [Doc 1] and [Doc 6].

issue: i am a professor in a college and wanted to setup a claude lti key for my students
subject: Claude for students
response: To set up the Claude LTI integration for your students in Canvas, follow these steps:  1. In Canvas, sign in as an administrator and navigate to **Admin -> Developer Keys**. Click '+ Developer Key' then '+ LTI Key.' 2. Enter the required details (Key Name: 'Claude LTI', Redirect URIs: https://claude.ai/lti/launch, etc.) as specified in [Doc 1]. 3. Under **Additional Settings**, set Privacy Level to **Public** and configure Placements (recommended: 'Course Navigation' and 'Assignment Edit'). 4. Save and toggle the state to **On**. 5. Install the app via **Admin -> Settings -> Apps -> View App Configurations -> + App** (Configuration Type: 'By Client ID'). 6. In Claude for Education, enable the integration under **Organization settings > Connectors** by entering your Canvas Domain, Client ID, and Deployment ID.  For full details, refer to [Set up the Claude LTI in Canvas by Instructure](https://support.claude.com/en/articles/11725453-set-up-the-claude-lti-in-canvas-by-instructure).
justification: The user is a professor (admin) asking for setup instructions, which are fully documented in [Doc 1] and [Doc 3]. No escalation needed as the user can self-serve.

issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
subject: Visa card minimum spend
response: In the US Virgin Islands, merchants may require a minimum transaction amount of US$10 for Visa credit card purchases. This is an exception to the general rule that merchants cannot set minimum or maximum amounts for Visa transactions. If you encounter an issue with a Visa debit card or a credit card transaction below $10, please notify your Visa card issuer.
justification: The user's question is answered by the policy in [Doc 1], which explicitly states the exception for US territories including the US Virgin Islands.
